rule_name: 亚临床甲亢患者长效治疗改善长期健康结局研究设计
source_text: Thyroid disease: assessment and management (NG145)© NICE 2019. All rights reserved. Subject to Notice of rights (https://www.nice.org.uk/terms-and-conditions#notice-of-rights).Page 27 of53http://guide.medlive.cn/4 L4 Long-term effectivong-term effectiveness and safety of reness and safety of radioactivadioactive iodinee iodinethertherapapy for hy for hyperthyperthyroidismyroidismWhat is the long-term clinical and cost effectiveness, including safety, of radioactive iodine forhyperthyroidism? In people with subclinical hyperthyroidism, does treatment (for example, withantithyroid drugs or radioiodine) to maintain long-term normal thyroid function improve long-termhealth outcomes (for example, cardiovascular outcomes, mortality, quality of life, osteoporosis andfractures, and neuropsychiatric outcomes such as depression and cognition)? A randomisedcontrolled trial comparing a strategy of long-term treatment to maintain normal thyroid functionwith no treatment. Outcomes should include mortality, cardiovascular events, fractures, andquality of life. Follow-up should be for at least 5 years. Screening for atrial fibrillation andassessment of depression and cognition should be included.

rule_name: 65岁以下亚临床甲减患者启动左甲状腺素试验指征

body.name: 患病
arguments: 亚临床甲状腺功能减退症

source_text: Consider a 6-month trial of levothyroxine for adults under 65 with subclinical hypothyroidism who have: a TSH above the reference range but lower than 10 mlU/litre on 2 separate occasions 3 months apart, and symptoms of hypothyroidism. If symptoms do not improve after starting levothyroxine, re-measure TSH and if the level remains raised, adjust the dose. If symptoms persist when serum TSH is within the reference range, consider stopping levothyroxine and follow the recommendations on monitoring untreated subclinical hypothyroidism and monitoring after stopping treatment.

rule_name: 左甲状腺素试验中症状未改善且TSH仍高时的剂量调整

body.name: 症状状态
